➡️ Next: Lines & catalogue · back to Proposals.

docs.title: Send by email
docs.text: Send the proposal to the client directly from the CRM.

docs.title: Step by step
docs.text:
1. On the proposal (or in the action bar), click Send by email.
  2. Choose the email template and the recipient (the client's email comes pre-filled).
  3. Subject and, if you wish, CC/BCC.
  4. Confirm the mode (chip at the top: Real send or Simulated).
  5. Send.

Your signature is added automatically at the end. On the free plan there is no PDF attachment — the proposal goes as a reference in the text (the client exports/receives the PDF separately).

Trainees send in simulated mode

Trainees always send in simulated mode (it logs to the history, does not deliver). It is safe to practise.

➡️ Next: Loss reason · back to Proposals.

docs.title: Lines & catalogue
docs.text: The proposal lines are the items — chosen from the product catalogue (there is no free-text description).

docs.title: Each line
docs.text:
• Item — chosen from the catalogue (grouped by family › subfamily). When you choose, it fills in the description (locked), price and VAT.
  • Quantity.
  • VAT — adjusts to the client's region. If a line has VAT from another region, "Correct to {region}" appears.
  • ➕ adds a line · 🗑️ removes.

docs.title: Totals (automatic)
docs.text:
Subtotal → client's loyalty discount → VAT (recalculated proportionally) → total.

No catalogue, no lines

Each line points to a product. First create the items in Products.

VAT by region

Mainland 23/13/6 · Madeira 22/12/5 · Azores 16/9/4. The VAT follows the proposal's client region.

➡️ Next: Pipeline (Kanban) · back to Proposals.

docs.title: Export PDF
docs.text: Generate the A4 document, ready to print or save.

docs.title: Step by step
docs.text:
1. Open the proposal → action bar.
  2. Preview — see the "sheet" as it will come out.
  3. Download PDF → in the browser's print window, choose Save as PDF.

The document uses the proposal's template (header, logo, quotation, totals, terms, signature) and the client's data. The footer stays fixed at the bottom of each page.

Switch template

Each proposal can use a different template. Try switching and exporting again to compare.

➡️ Next: Send by email · back to Proposals.

docs.title: Loss reason & reopen
docs.text: When a proposal does not move forward, you record why — for later analysis.

docs.title: Recording the loss
docs.text:
When you change the status to Lost (in the modal or by dragging to the Lost column), the app asks for:

  • Reason — Price / Competition / Timing / No budget / No response / Needs changed / Features / Other.
  • Free note (optional).

What it's for

The reasons feed the Loss analysis in Analytics — you see the biggest cause of loss in your base.

docs.title: Reopen
docs.text:
Changed your mind? On a Lost (or Won) proposal, the Reopen button puts it back into Negotiation.

➡️ Back to Proposals · see Analytics.

docs.title: Pipeline (Kanban)
docs.text:
Proposals are organised by status, in columns you can drag.

Lead → Qualification → Negotiation → Won → Lost

Each proposal is a card; drag between columns to change status.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
• Dragging a card between columns changes the status (records who and when).
  • Each column shows the total at the bottom.
  • Dropping into Lost prompts for the loss reason.
  • Won/Lost proposals are locked (read-only).

docs.title: Reopen
docs.text:
On a closed proposal, Reopen puts it back into Negotiation and unlocks the lines.

List view

Prefer a table? Switch to List at the top of the module — same data, a different read.

➡️ Next: Export PDF · back to Proposals.

docs.title: Getting started — Trainer
docs.text: In just a few minutes you have your class up and running. There are three steps.

docs.title: 1. Create the class
docs.text:
1. Open iefpcrm.cr0x.org.
  2. On the home screen, click “I’m a trainer — create a new class”.
  3. Fill in:
    • IEFP trainer email (forNNNN@formacao.iefp.pt) — this is what authorizes the creation.
    • First name and surname.
    • Class number (8 digits) and, optionally, a name for the class.
    • Your username and password.
  4. Click Create class and enter.

Save the recovery code

Right after you create the class a recovery code appears (e.g. A1B2-C3D4-E5F6), shown only once. It is how you recover your password if you forget it. Write it down somewhere safe.

You automatically become the Administrator of your class — only you, no one else.

docs.title: 2. Add the trainees
docs.text:
Inside the class, go to Settings → Class trainees. You have two ways:

  • Bulk import (Excel/CSV) — download the Template, fill it in and import. It shows you a preview with errors flagged before saving.
  • Individual — fill in the form and click Add to class.

Full details in Manage the class.

docs.title: 3. Share access
docs.text:
Share the link with the number with the class:

https://iefpcrm.cr0x.org/?t=12345678

Each trainee opens the link and signs in with their own username and password.

That’s it!

From here on, each trainee works in their own sandbox. When they submit their work, you receive everything in Submission & validation.

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• Manage the class — import, access, reset passwords
  • Submission & validation — review and grade work
  • Email & Brand — sending mode and white-label

docs.title: Getting started — Trainee
docs.text: Welcome! Here are the essentials to get you started.

docs.title: 1. Enter the class
docs.text:
1. Open the link your trainer gave you (it has the class number): 
    https://iefpcrm.cr0x.org/?t=12345678
  2. Type your username and your password.
  3. Click Enter.

Don’t have the link?

On the home screen you can type the class number in the dedicated field and click Enter. If the number doesn’t exist, the app warns you.

docs.title: 2. Get to know your space
docs.text:
Each trainee has their own sandbox — a copy of the CRM just for you, with sample data already loaded. You can create, edit and delete freely: it doesn’t affect your classmates.

Navigation is on the left, grouped (Main, Marketing & Analysis, Compliance, System).

See what each module does in Working in the CRM.

docs.title: 3. Change your password (recommended)
docs.text:
1. Click your name (bottom-left corner) → opens My profile.
  2. In Current password, type the current one; in New password, the new one.
  3. Save changes.

Forgot your password?

See Recover your password — you can ask the trainer to reset it.

docs.title: 4. Submit your work
docs.text:
When you finish the exercise, click “Submit my work” (at the bottom of the sidebar). You have a maximum of 2 submissions.

Step by step in Submit your work.

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• Working in the CRM — the modules explained
  • Submit your work — how to hand in
  • Recover your password

docs.title: GDPR & Pedagogy
docs.text:
The GDPR module is the core of the UFCD 10870 — CRM Administration (data protection). Everything respects the date filter and there is a printable compliance report.

Consents, data subject requests, RoPA and breaches — in a single module.

docs.title: Module areas
docs.text:
• Consents

    Legal basis, purpose and collection channel.

    Open

  • Data subject requests (DSAR)

    Access, rectification, erasure, portability…

    Open

  • RoPA

    Records of Processing Activities.

    Open

  • Data breaches

    Recording and managing incidents.

    Open

  • Data Subject Portal

    The public portal for consents and rights.

    Open

docs.title: Link to the UFCD
docs.text:
UFCD Where it is practised 10868 — CRM Analytics Dashboard, Analytics, Campaigns, segmentation, funnel, CAC/LTV 10870 — CRM Administration GDPR (consents, DSAR, RoPA, breaches), users and permissions

Pedagogical tooltips

Throughout the app, the ⓘ icon brings up the definition of the concept (consent, data subject, breach, RoPA…) and the reference to the slide page.

docs.title: Consents
docs.text: Record of each data subject's consent to the processing of their data.

docs.title: Each consent
docs.text:
• Customer/Data subject it relates to.
  • Purpose — marketing, newsletter, relationship management…
  • Legal basis — Consent, Contract, Legitimate interest, Legal obligation…
  • Collection channel and Date.
  • Status — Active / Withdrawn.

Valid consent

It must be freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous. The data subject can withdraw it at any time.

docs.title: Step by step
docs.text:
1. GDPR → Consents tab.
  2. + Consent → choose data subject, purpose, legal basis and channel.
  3. Save.
  4. To withdraw, change the status to Withdrawn (or the data subject does so on the Portal).

➡️ Next: Data subject requests · back to GDPR.

docs.title: Data subject requests (DSAR)
docs.text: DSARs (Data Subject Access Requests) are the requests by which a data subject exercises their rights.

docs.title: Rights (request types)
docs.text: Right What it is Access To know what data exists about you Rectification To correct incorrect data Erasure "Right to be forgotten" Portability To receive the data in a reusable format Objection To object to a processing activity Restriction To restrict the processing

docs.title: Each request
docs.text: • Data subject, Type, Date, Deadline (1 month) and Status (Received / In progress / Completed).

docs.title: Step by step
docs.text:
1. GDPR → Requests tab.
  2. + Request (or it arrives via the Data Subject Portal).
  3. Handle the request and update the status.

Deadline

You have 1 month to respond. Requests that are overdue or due soon (≤7 days) show up in the Alerts.

➡️ Next: RoPA · back to GDPR.

docs.title: Data Subject Portal
docs.text: A public page where any data subject can review their consents and exercise their rights — without entering the app.

docs.title: What it is for
docs.text:
• The data subject sees the consents they have given.
  • Withdraws a consent.
  • Submits a request (access, erasure, portability…) that goes straight into the Requests (DSAR).

docs.title: How to access it
docs.text:
It opens at #portal (the Data Subject Portal link in the menu/footer). It is public: designed to be shared with customers.

Link to the module

Everything that comes in through the portal appears in the trainer's GDPR module — it is the full circuit of exercising rights, from the data subject to whoever processes the data.

➡️ Back to GDPR.

docs.title: RoPA — Records of Processing Activities
docs.text: The RoPA (Records of Processing Activities, Article 30 of the GDPR) lists what the organisation does with personal data.

docs.title: Each activity
docs.text:
• Name of the activity (e.g. Customer management, Newsletter).
  • Purpose and Legal basis.
  • Categories of data subjects and of data.
  • Recipients and any transfers.
  • Retention period.

docs.title: Step by step
docs.text:
1. GDPR → RoPA tab.
  2. + Activity → fill in the fields.
  3. Save — it enters the compliance report.

Why it is mandatory

The RoPA is the map of data processing. It is the first document a supervisory authority asks for in an audit.

➡️ Next: Data breaches · back to GDPR.

docs.title: Data breaches
docs.text: Record of security incidents that affect personal data (loss, unauthorised access, leak…).

docs.title: Each breach
docs.text:
• Date and Description of the incident.
  • Affected data and number of data subjects.
  • Severity and measures taken.
  • Notified to the CNPD? and to the data subjects?

docs.title: Step by step
docs.text:
1. GDPR → Breaches tab.
  2. + Breach → describe the incident and the affected data.
  3. Record the measures and the notification status.

The 72-hour deadline

A risk breach must be notified to the CNPD within 72 hours. If there is a high risk, the data subjects must also be informed.

➡️ Next: Data Subject Portal · back to GDPR.

docs.title: Guided tutorial: set up the class in 10 minutes
docs.text:
In this tutorial

Goal: create the class, import the trainees, share access and be ready to receive submissions. · Duration: ~10 min · For: Trainer.

docs.title: Before you start
docs.text:
• [x] You have your IEFP institutional email (forNNNN@formacao.iefp.pt).
  • [x] You have the list of trainees (first name, surname, email).

docs.title: Step 1 · Create the class
docs.text:
1. Open iefpcrm.cr0x.org → “I’m a trainer — create a new class”.
  2. Fill in IEFP email, name, class number (8 digits), username and password → Create class and enter.

Save the recovery code

It appears once — write it down. It is your way to recover the password.

📷 Screenshot

docs.title: Step 2 · Prepare the list (Excel)
docs.text:
1. Settings → Class trainees → Excel/CSV Template.
  2. Fill in one row per trainee: first name · surname · email · username · password.

Password

If you leave the password column empty, the initial password is used (field on the page) — the same for everyone; each trainee changes it later.

docs.title: Step 3 · Import with preview
docs.text:
1. Click Import list and choose the file.
  2. Check the preview: OK rows in green, errors flagged in red.
  3. Import N valid.

Result

The trainees appear in the list, ready to enter.

📷 Screenshot

(Capture import-preview.png — the preview table.)

docs.title: Step 4 · Share access
docs.text:
Send the class the link with the number:

https://iefpcrm.cr0x.org/?t=12345678

Each trainee enters with their own username and password.

docs.title: Step 5 · (Optional) Enable real email sending
docs.text: In Settings → Real email sending, turn on Enable real sending and run Send test. (The keys are fixed; you only manage the mode.)

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• When the trainees hand in, you go to Submission & validation — review, generate PDF, download.
  • Customize the Brand if you want the CRM with a different identity.

docs.title: Guided tutorial: from zero to your first proposal
docs.text:
In this tutorial

Goal: create a company, a contact and an item, build a proposal, export the PDF and submit it. · Duration: ~12 min · For: Trainee (and anyone getting to know the CRM).

docs.title: Before you start
docs.text:
• [x] You are inside your class (you entered through the ?t=… link).
  • [x] You see the sidebar on the left (Dashboard, Companies, Contacts…).

If you haven’t entered yet, see Getting started — Trainee.

docs.title: Step 1 · Create the company
docs.text:
1. In the sidebar, click Companies.
  2. Click + Company.
  3. Fill in Name (e.g. Hotel Sol & Mar, Lda.) and the NIPC (the app validates it).
  4. Choose the Region (e.g. Madeira) — it will set the VAT further on.
  5. Save.

Result

The company appears in the list. Click it to see the 360º view (no contacts yet for now).

📷 Screenshot

(Capture empresas.png — the company form filled in.)

docs.title: Step 2 · Create the contact
docs.text:
1. Click Contacts → + Contact.
  2. First name and Surname (e.g. Ana Costa).
  3. In Company, choose the one you created; fill in the Role.
  4. Email, NIF (validated) and the Segment (e.g. SME).
  5. Confirm the Region (it inherits the VAT logic). Save.

Loyalty

The loyalty level (Bronze/Silver/Gold) adjusts with purchase volume and gives an automatic discount on proposals.

docs.title: Step 3 · Create the item (catalog)
docs.text:
1. Click Products.
  2. + Family (e.g. Software) → + Subfamily (e.g. Licenses).
  3. + Item: name (CRM Pro License), family/subfamily, price (e.g. €79) and VAT. Save.

Why the catalog first?

In proposals, the lines are chosen from the catalog — it ensures consistent prices and VAT.

docs.title: Step 4 · Build the proposal
docs.text:
1. Click Proposals → + Proposal.
  2. Customer: choose Ana Costa → the loyalty discount and the region’s VAT now apply.
  3. Title: CRM licensing proposal.
  4. Line: choose the item CRM Pro License → description/price/VAT fill in; set Quantity 5.
  5. Note the Totals block (subtotal, discount, VAT, total).
  6. Save.

VAT notice

If the customer’s region doesn’t match the line’s VAT, “Correct to {region}” appears — click it to align.

📷 Screenshot

docs.title: Step 5 · Export the PDF
docs.text:
1. Open the proposal → action bar → Preview (see the A4 sheet).
  2. Download PDF → in the print dialog, choose Save as PDF.

Document look

The layout comes from the Proposal Template. Try switching templates and exporting again.

docs.title: Step 6 · Move through the pipeline and submit
docs.text:
1. In Proposals (Kanban view), drag the card to Negotiation and then Won.
  2. Set your signature in your profile (bottom-left corner) — it is required to hand in.
  3. Click Submit my work → confirm and Submit.

You completed the flow!

You created data, a proposal, a PDF and made the submission — the full cycle of a CRM. 🎉

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• Go deeper into each screen in Features.
  • Explore GDPR (consents, data subject requests).
  • See the Analytics with your proposal already counted.
